title: Native bf16 Float impl (Plan A, follow-up to Plan B PoC)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

Follow-up to the Plan B hybrid storage issue. After the PoC profiling data is in and we know bf16 is the right target precision, do a proper bf16 Float trait impl.

Work:
- Implement Float for half::bf16 in ferrotorch-core/src/dtype.rs. Cover: arithmetic ops, transcendentals (SIMD where possible, fall back to f32 via upcast), epsilon/tiny/min/max constants, rng support.
- Extend ferrotorch-ufunc / SIMD paths to have bf16 variants where profitable; elsewhere upcast-compute-downcast.
- ferrotorch-gpu: native bf16 kernels for elementwise ops, layernorm, rmsnorm, softmax. Currently bf16 only appears in the matmul upcast path.
- StateDict<bf16> load path in safetensors_io (avoid the f32 round-trip).
- Mixed-precision training: bf16 weights + f32 optimizer state (standard mixed-precision recipe).

Acceptance:
- Tensor<bf16> compiles and runs end-to-end.
- Llama 3 8B loaded as StateDict<bf16> directly, no f32 intermediate.
- Training on a toy task in bf16 converges equivalently to f32 baseline (no loss divergence).

Non-blocker for PoC. File when the PoC produces profiling data and we decide bf16 is worth doing properly.
